summary: Tested full Nominations workflow (backend + frontend). All 10 backend tests passed (POST seeds status_history, GET requires admin & normalizes legacy 'new'->'pending', PUT updates status with history append, internal_notes/interview/rejection_reason persistence, invalid status -> 400, missing token -> 403, non-existent id -> 404). All UI flows passed: login, status filter tabs (all 9 with counts), search, field filter, clear filters, pending-card actions (Approve/Request Info/Reject/Details), Detail modal (status pill, status change dropdown, internal notes save, status history), Email modal (prefilled subject/body, To, Open Email/Copy WhatsApp/Cancel), Approve flow transitions card to Approved status, Approved actions (Send Contact/Schedule/Reject), Schedule modal with datetime picker + link + send-email checkbox transitions to Scheduled, Scheduled actions (Mark Done/Reschedule/Send Reminder), Mark Done -> in_production with only Ready-to-Publish button visible, Ready-to-Publish opens Add Story form with name/age/field/achievement/city/state pre-filled, Reject modal with reason dropdown + notes + send-email checkbox transitions to Rejected. 0 JS errors observed.
critical_code_review_comments: NominationsPanel.jsx is ~813 lines — consider splitting DetailModal/EmailModal/RejectModal/ScheduleModal into separate files for maintainability (>700 line guideline).

Email modal 'Copy for WhatsApp' silently calls onSent() which advances status even when the user just copied for WhatsApp without confirming send — acceptable but worth documenting; admin may not have actually sent anything.
list_nominations() limit is 500 — fine for now but add pagination if volumes grow.

follow_up/send_reminder actions reuse same template and do NOT change status (postStatus = nom.status); UI does not visibly indicate the email was 'sent', only adds nothing — consider adding a contact/follow-up timestamp field for audit.
Backend PUT does not update the timestamp on status_history when only internal_notes/interview_date change without status — fine since history is status-only; just confirming intent.
No backend endpoint to delete a nomination — likely intentional, but flag for future.
Schedule modal: interview_date is stored as locale string via new Date(date).toLocaleString() — non-ISO. Consider storing ISO and formatting on display so the value can be reused programmatically.
seed_data_creation: Created 2 fresh nominations (UI_Test_Nominee_0/1) via POST /api/nominations to drive UI tests. Also one TEST_Nominee from pytest fixture (ends in 'rejected' state).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Test data with prefix 'UI_Test_Nominee_' and 'TEST_Nominee' / 'TEST_Nominator' exists in DB. Nomination workflow is fully functional end-to-end. If retesting, simply re-run /app/backend/tests/test_nominations.py — fixtures create a fresh TEST_ nomination per module.
